REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT RAY YEGANEH NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMLPAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 IS DENIED. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE FIRST AMENDED PETITION IS LARGELY INCOMPREHENSIBLE. NOTWITHSTANDING THE UNCERTAINTY OF THE PETITION, DEFENDANT FILED THIS MOTION SEEKING TO STRIKE THE PETITION AND 4 CAUSES OF ACTION THEREIN. DEFENDANT DOES NOT IDENTIFY THE PURPORTED CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT FURTHER DOES NOT IDENTIFY ANY CLAIMS UPON WHICH RELIEF MAY BE GRANTED, THAT DEFENDANT BELIEVES ARE SUBJECT TO ANTI-SLAPP. FOR EXAMPLE, DEFENDANT DOES NOT ADDRESS HOW THE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND WHAT THAT PROTECTED ACTIVITY MAY BE IN RELATION TO THE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION POINTS TO ALLEGATIONS ON PAGE 4 AND 5 OF THE COMPLAINT. THOSE ALLEGATIONS APPEAR TO STATE THAT A LIEN FOR A CERTAIN AMOUNT WAS RECODED. DEFENDANT CITES TO NO AUTHORITY AND PROVIDES NO ARGUMENT TO SUPPORT THEIR ASSERTION THAT THE RECORDATION OF THIS LIEN IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY WITHIN THE MEANING ON CCP 425.16(E)(1)-(4). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Comlpaint Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
Off Calendar Sep-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: In-Person Appearance

Stephanie Chu, plaintiff in pro per
Shannon Kwei, Mandarin interpreter

Ms Chu is not awared of the tentative ruling prior to the hearing and did not notice the opposing party.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAY YEGANEH NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMLPAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 is DENIED. The Court notes that the First Amended Petition is largely incomprehensible. Notwithstanding the uncertainty of the Petition, Defendant filed this motion seeking to strike the petition and 4 causes of action therein. Defendant does not identify the purported causes of action. Defendant further does not identify any claims upon which relief may be granted, that defendant believes are subject to ANTI-SLAPP. For example, Defendant does not address how the claim for breach of contract arises out of protected activity and what that protected activity may be in relation to the claim for breach of contract. Defendant's notice of Motion points to allegations on page 4 and 5 of the Complaint. Those allegations appear to state that a lien for a certain amount was recoded. Defendant cites to no authority and provides no argument to support their assertion that the recordation of this lien is protected activity within the meaning on CCP 425.16(e)(1)-(4).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
